contributor: university of maryland, college park, md
description: 8 CIO War Relief Committee Helps Armed Forces When the 1941 convention of the CIO established the National CIO War Relief Committee it signified ClOs determine tion not to be content merely with pro ducing for victory allimportant as that may bobut to contribute money for services for the armed forces During the past two years the CIO has raised through its war relief committee some 50 million dollars to help service the varied needs of our allies and our own fighting men Thanks to these millions of dinner pall dollars given by CIO worker to the USO and the Red Cross hundreds of thousands of our boys In uniform are finding muchneeded recreational fa cilities close to wherever they may be stationed Many a dayroom in domestic army camps has been furnished and equipped with ath letic supplies and musical instruments bought with contributions of CIO members In cities like Philadelphia Pittsburgh and Harrisburg hundreds of soldiers nightly en joy dancing and refreshments at labor sponsored laborconstructed laboroperated USO canteens And on a battlefield somewhere over seas the lives of American fighters are being saved by blood donated by CIO workers A less dramatic nevertheless valuable service rendered by
